Section.name: UNCLASSIFIED
Section.Content: Introduction The content of this service description (Chapter 1) is used to compile a service catalog that is used in the contract with the client. This introduction and document history below are for internal use only. It is allowed to merge Chapter 1 with other service descriptions into a single service catalog. Modifying the content of Chapter 1 for individual clients is not allowed. (If necessary, any adjustments can only be made with approval from the responsible service owner of product manager.) Document history Version Date Comment Author 0.9 12-10-2023 Initial document Lodewijk van der Sloot

Section.name: Service summary
Section.Content: The Windows OS management service includes the technical management of a Windows server – physical or virtual.

Section.name: Standard service components
Section.Content: Operating system management This Service includes technical management of the Windows operating system running on a physical or virtual server. Cegeka shall be responsible in providing the following Services with the objective to maintain system availability within the defined SLA for the respective OS: Incident & Problem Management Based on incidents logged by monitoring, Cegeka proactively takes the following actions: User & Access Management For domain joined servers, regular user and access management will be configured and controlled via Microsoft Active Directory. This shall be configured as outlined in Cegeka’s Best Practice document for both ADDS setup and AD Delegation. For Workgroup servers, typically stand-alone machines part of DMZ therefore not limited to, a particular set-up will be foreseen which will be consequently roll-out on every stand-alone machine. Security Antivirus Patching & Lifecycle management Patches in scope The patches in scope for this service are related to the Windows server operating system. The following categorization is applied: Cegeka installs all patches related to ‘critical updates’ and ‘security updates’, in agreement with <KLANT>. All other patches are out of scope. Cegeka implements interim patches issued via security alerts through Cegeka’s emergency change process; these may be “urgent critical” patches or patches related to an ongoing incident. Patch frequency The managed Windows OS service ensures that a managed server is updated to the most recent patches every month. This frequency may be different depending on specific separate agreements, this can be provided as an option in this service. Service packs In accordance with the Microsoft Product Support Lifecycle, the policy concerning service packs is the following: When the support of a Windows server version ends, then the support for all service packs of that version will end. In such a case, Cegeka is willing to discuss with <KLANT> the need to continue to provide support on a best effort basis. Supported versions Cegeka applies a support version lifecycle policy on the managed Windows server service, for which the Microsoft support lifecycle is used:

Section.name: Optional service components
Section.Content: Backup & restore management This feature is the management of the OS backups and restores from an operational perspective. It is dependent on the presence of the shared backup service. This option should typically be standard for production systems but might be left out for Test/Dev Environments. License management of the OS and underlying components This feature is the management of the OS licenses for the managed system or components installed on the system. For this option to be available Cegeka needs certain authorizations with respect to the licenses in question, which will depend on the exact product. Non-standard patch frequency Specific patch frequencies can be agreed for named systems. Custom activity list This feature provides the possibility for <KLANT> to define custom activities (e.g. in the form of a workbook) to be executed by Cegeka.

Section.name: Eligibility and Prerequisites
Section.Content: Conditions Out of scope The following patches are out of scope: The Cegeka technical architect assigned to <KLANT> discusses the out of scope patches on a regular basis and on request of <KLANT>. Out of scope patches for which it is decided to implement them and not a part of another in scope service, will be implemented through separate RFC’s but using the regular patching process or emergency change process. Management of the applications running on the windows servers is not in scope with Cegeka. In the Service Delivery Plan, specific agreements need to be documented on the installation and upgrade of applications on servers and the required permissions to do so. The following options exist: Similar agreements need to be made and documented in the SDP with respect to the management of the applications on the servers, especially concerning the permissions that are required for this application management. Maintenance & Licenses <KLANT> is responsible for managing licensing compliancy for the OS licenses needed to run the windows operating system. For servers running on the Cegeka Cloud platform, Cegeka will provide full management of the Windows OS licensing Trough the SPLA program and customers are relieved of responsibility for license compliance for the Widows server OS for all versions of Windows Server Operating System still actively supported by Microsoft under their normal, not extended, support license agreement. From the moment an OS version leaves MS normal support regime under any circumstance it’s the customers responsibility to provide Cegeka with licenses which provide extended support to remain eligible to install critical security patches. Responsibility matrix Activity Customer Cegeka Operating System Create OS build for servers I R, A Installing system software (OS, monitoring tools) R, A Distribution (installation) of packages to target systems (business applications) R, A Maintain system documentation I R, A Creating file systems and other movements at storage level R, A Clean up of root file system and/or other file systems R, A System (super) user creating/mutate I R, A Rolling out system release/service packs/patches R, A Identifying technical system errors and problems I R, A Technical troubleshooting system errors and incidents I R, A Performance tuning analysis I R, A System parameterization for performance (tuning) I R, A Starting/stopping OS specific batches R, A Creating and/or restoring backups I R, A Make Changes to system configuration I R, A Connecting PV system on Network R, A Configure network parameters on OS I R, A Windows Software & Patch Management Follow up patches of the OS I R, A Evaluate and assess patch effect on infrastructure I R, A Test patch deployment R, A C Decide on deployment of patches of the OS R, A C Propose, prepare and advise on deployment of major updates of the OS C, I R, A Approve deployment of major updates of the OS, according to operational procedures R, A C Distribute (install) patches or releases of the OS to server systems R, A Definition of a non-standard patch frequency C, I R, A Antivirus Endpoint Protection Agent Deployment R, A Ensure that anti-virus definitions are up to date R, A Endpoint Protection Customization (exclusions) C, I R, A Monitoring Monitoring Agent Deployment R, A Monitoring Plan - OS Level (Services) C, I R, A Monitor, report and solve OS system errors and problems I R, A Monitor Capacity (CPU, Storage, Network,….) R, A

Section.name: Order & billing information
Section.Content: Part numbers O.0120_0020_0010_C_S.000 - Physical – OS Gold O.0120_0020_0020_C_S.000 - Physical – OS Silver O.0120_0020_0030_C_S.000 - Physical – OS Bronze O.0120_0020_0040_C_S.000 - Virtual – OS Gold O.0120_0020_0050_C_S.000 - Virtual – OS Silver O.0120_0020_0060_C_S.000 - Virtual – OS Bronze Measurement A report is generated on a monthly basis, this report is based on the CMDB and lists all servers and service details for which a Cegeka datacentre – Managed Windows OS Service is delivered. Billing The units are billed against the price of their respective part numbers. (“Price per Unit” x “Quantity of Virtual – Gold Units”) + (“Price per Unit” x “Quantity of Virtual – Silver Units”) + (“Price per Unit” x “Quantity of Virtual – Bronze Units”).
